yeasted-coffee-cake.name: Yeasted Coffee Cake

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal kosher salt; for table salt
ingredients.prep: use half as much by volume

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal kosher salt; for table salt
ingredients.prep: use half as much by volume

instructions: In a small saucepan, whisk milk and flour to combine. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until mixture thickens to a stretchy pudding-like consistency, 2 to 3 minutes.

Using a flexible spatula, scrape hot tangzhong into the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the dough hook attachment; let cool until just warm to the touch, about 10 minutes. Add cold milk to tangzhong, followed by the flour, butter, egg yolks, sugar, yeast, and salt. Mix on low speed until dough is smooth, supple, and elastic, 12 to 15 minutes. (The dough should pass the “windowpane” test: Pull off a golf-ball-sized piece of dough and, using two hands, stretch it as thin as you can without the dough ripping. If you can see through it, the dough is ready to rest. If not, continue kneading for another 2 to 4 minutes or until the dough passes this test.)
Loosely cover bowl with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let sit at warm room temperature (75 to 80ºF; 24 to 27ºC) until nearly doubled in size, 60 to 90 minutes.

In a small bowl, whisk brown sugar, flour, ground cinnamon, and salt to combine. Add pecans, followed by the melted butter, using your hands to toss and gently press until the mixture is sandy. Set aside.

Grease a 9-inch aluminum cake pan with nonstick spray. Line the pan with two 15-inch by 3-inch strips of parchment paper, overlapping them to form an “X” in the bottom of the pan. (There should be about 3 inches of overhang on the edges.)

When dough has risen, lightly flour top of dough and transfer to a lightly-floured work surface. Roll dough into a 9- by 16-inch rectangle. Scatter the filling evenly over the surface of the dough, and starting with the long end, roll the dough up as tightly as possible. Carefully pinch dough together along the seam to seal, and gently pat log to ensure even thickness; brush off any excess flour from the dough.

Gently coil dough around itself to create a circular shape. Cover with a clean, damp kitchen towel and let dough rest for 5 to 10 minutes to allow gluten to relax. Roll dough into a 9-inch wide circle about 3/4-inch thick, then transfer dough to prepared pan. Loosely cover with plastic wrap or damp kitchen towel and let rise at warm room temperature until dough appears slightly puffed, about 30 minutes. Tightly wrap cake pan with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 2 hours and up to 12.

When ready to bake, remove dough from refrigerator and let rise at warm room temperature until roughly doubled in size, dough is about 1 3/4-inches tall, and bounces back slowly when gently pressed, 1 to 2 hours.
Adjust oven rack to middle position and preheat to 350ºF (175ºC).
Firmly press pecan halves into the surface of the dough, arranging them in a circular pattern around the dough. (Dough will deflate slightly—this is fine.)

Bake until light golden brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of the cake registers 88°C (88ºC), 20 to 25 minutes. Remove cake from oven and, using a pastry brush, immediately brush cake with melted butter. Lift the parchment to remove the cake from the pan and transfer it to a wire cooling rack. Let cool until slightly warm, 30 to 40 minutes.

In a medium bowl, whisk confectioners sugar, milk, vanilla extract, and salt until smooth. Glaze should be thick but pourable; if glaze seems too thick, whisk in milk, 1 teaspoon at a time, until glaze is the right consistency.
Using a spoon, drizzle glaze evenly over cake. Let set for 10 minutes before slicing with a serrated knife and serving.

yogurt-cake-with-spring-onion-lezzet.name: Yogurt Cake With Spring Onion
instructions: 1. Add yogurt, lor cheese and olive oil on a deep coarse crumb.
2. With the help of mixer, add 2-3 minutes of whipping, fine chopped green onions and mix well with fork.
3. Mix the carbonate and flour in a separate container and adding azar to the mixture with onion again.
4. Ensure that the materials are thoroughly mixed with each other and transfer it to the cake mold.
5. Preheated 180 degree bake up to 40-45 minutes in the oven.
6. Mix yogurt and fine chopped dereot for yogurt sauce in a container.
7. Wait for the cake you remove from the oven to be warm and go on the yogurt sauce.
8. Serve by slitting.

yogurt-with-raisins-cucumber-and-walnuts.name: Yogurt With Raisins, Cucumber, And Walnuts

ingredients.item: Crushed dried rose petals; plus more for serving

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Toast walnuts on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing occasionally, until golden brown, 8–10 minutes. Let cool, then finely chop.

Mix walnuts, cucumber, yogurt, raisins, green garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, 1 Tbsp. mint, 1 Tbsp. oil, and 2 tsp. rose petals, if using, in a medium bowl to combine; season with salt and pepper. If yogurt looks thick, stir in milk as needed.
Serve topped with Aleppo-style pepper and more dried mint and rose petals and drizzled with more oil.
Do Ahead: Yogurt can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

yukon-gold-potato-and-wild-mushroom-grat.name: Yukon Gold Potato And Wild Mushroom Gratin
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. Melt butter in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and sauté until brown and soft, about 6 minutes. Generously butter 11x7x2-inch baking dish. Peel and cut potatoes into 1/8-inch-thick slices. Arrange 2 layers of potatoes in prepared dish. Spoon 1/3 of mushrooms over potatoes. Sprinkle with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.

Repeat 2 more times with potatoes, mushrooms, salt, and pepper. Pour cream over potatoes. Cover with foil and bake 45 minutes. DO AHEAD: Can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature. Uncover and continue baking until golden brown and set, about 20 minutes longer (30 minutes if room temperature). Let stand 10 minutes before serving.

yuzu-chicken-piccata.name: Yuzu Chicken Piccata

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
Low-sodium chicken broth or  0.667 cup water mixed with  0.75 tsp. chicken bouillon paste
. (or more) yuzu juice (from at least a 3 oz. bottle or larger)

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced on a deep diagonal

instructions: Place ¾ cup (94 g) all-purpose flour in a shallow bowl. Working one at a time, lightly pound 4 small skinless, boneless chicken breasts (about 6 oz. each) between 2 sheets of plastic wrap with a rolling pin (faster than a meat mallet) to about ¼" thick. Season all over with kosher salt and freshly ground pepper, then dredge in flour, shaking off excess; transfer to a large plate.

Heat 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Working in 2 batches and adding more oil between batches if needed, cook chicken until golden brown underneath, about 4 minutes. Turn and cook on other side until chicken is nearly cooked through, 1–2 minutes. Transfer back to plate.

Reduce heat to medium; add remaining 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil. Add 6 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and cook, stirring, until slightly softened, about 30 seconds. Pour in ½ cup dry white wine and simmer, stirring occasionally and scraping up any browned bits, until almost completely reduced, 1–2 minutes.

Increase heat to medium-high and add ⅔ cup low-sodium chicken broth or ⅔ cup water mixed with ¾ tsp. chicken bouillon paste, 3 Tbsp. unsalted butter, and ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt. Cook, stirring often, until butter is melted and combined, about 2 minutes. Stir in 2 tsp. soy sauce and 5 Tbsp. yuzu juice. Return chicken to skillet; cook, spooning sauce over, until chicken is cooked through and sauce is reduced slightly, about 3 minutes. Season with more salt and add more yuzu juice if needed. Add remaining 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter; cook, swirling pan, until sauce is glossy. Remove from heat; season with more pepper.
Toss 3 scallions, thinly sliced on a deep diagonal, a pinch of salt, and remaining 1 Tbsp. yuzu juice in a small bowl to coat.
Scatter dressed scallions over chicken in skillet. Serve with country-style bread alongside for dipping into sauce.
